content_id: Dan Allah bebas dari segala kekurangan dan sekutu, dan aku bebas dari orang-orang yang mempersekutukan apa pun dalam ibadah bersama-Nya.' [Surat Yusuf: 108]. Pengetahuan yang pasti (bashirah) adalah berkenaan dengan apa yang ia serukan; dan berarti bahwa pendakwah memiliki pengetahuan tentang hukum-hukum Syariat, dan bagaimana dakwah harus dilakukan, serta mengetahui kondisi orang-orang yang menjadi sasaran dakwah. Bidang-bidang dakwah itu banyak: Menyeru kepada Allah dengan memberikan ceramah dan kuliah; menyeru kepada Allah melalui penulisan artikel; menyeru kepada Allah melalui halaqah-halaqah ilmu; menyeru kepada Allah dengan menulis buku-buku yang menyebarkan agama; dan menyeru kepada Allah dalam perkumpulan yang dibentuk untuk tujuan-tujuan tertentu. Jika seseorang duduk dalam sebuah pertemuan di mana ia datang karena undangan makan, misalnya, maka ini adalah kesempatan untuk berdakwah kepada Allah, Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung. Namun hal itu harus dilakukan dengan cara yang tidak membosankan atau mengganggu orang-orang. Ini dapat dicapai dengan mengajukan suatu masalah ilmu kepada mereka yang hadir agar sebuah diskusi dapat dimulai, dan sudah diketahui bahwa diskusi, tanya jawab adalah manfaat besar dalam memahami apa yang Allah turunkan kepada Rasul-Nya dan dalam membuat orang lain memahaminya. Ini mungkin lebih efektif daripada memberikan ceramah atau kuliah yang tidak terputus. Menyeru kepada Allah adalah kewajiban para rasul, dan merupakan jalan orang-orang yang mengikuti mereka dengan baik. Sehingga ketika seseorang mengetahui Dia yang harus ia sembah, dan mengetahui Nabinya, dan agamanya, dan Allah telah menganugerahinya dengan itu dan membimbingnya kepadanya, maka dia harus berjuang untuk menyelamatkan saudara-saudaranya dengan menyeru mereka kepada Allah dan harus senang mendengar kabar gembira yang Nabi berikan kepada Ali ibn Abi Thalib, semoga Allah meridhainya, pada hari Khaybar: 'Pergilah tanpa tergesa-gesa hingga engkau mencapai wilayah mereka. Kemudian ajaklah mereka kepada Islam, dan beritahukan kepada mereka hak-hak Allah Yang Maha Tinggi yang mengikat mereka; karena demi Allah, sesungguhnya Allah membimbing satu orang melaluimu lebih baik bagimu daripada unta merah.' [Shahih al-Bukhari jilid 5, hal. 368-369, no. 521]. Beliau bersabda dalam riwayat yang dilaporkan oleh Muslim: 'Barangsiapa mengajak kepada petunjuk maka baginya pahala seperti pahala-pahala orang yang mengikutinya dan itu tidak akan mengurangi pahala mereka sedikit pun, dan barangsiapa mengajak kepada kesesatan akan menanggung dosa seperti dosa-dosa orang yang mengikutinya, dan itu tidak akan mengurangi dosa mereka sedikit pun.' [Shahih Muslim jilid 3, hal. 1050, no. 4665]. (iv) Bersabar dan menanggung segala gangguan yang ditemui di atas jalan itu. [14] As-Sabr (Kesabaran) adalah menahan diri pada ketaatan kepada Allah; dan menahan diri dari kemaksiatan kepada Allah, dan dari marah terhadap apa yang Allah tetapkan. Sehingga seseorang harus selalu bersemangat dan berenergi dalam berdakwah kepada agama Allah, meskipun menghadapi perlakuan buruk. Ini karena menyakiti orang-orang yang menyeru kepada kebenaran adalah sifat yang ditemukan dalam diri manusia, kecuali bagi orang-orang yang Allah bimbing. Allah berfirman kepada Nabi-Nya: 'Sesungguhnya rasul-rasul sebelum kamu pun telah didustakan (pula), tetapi mereka sabar terhadap pendustaan dan penganiayaan (yang dilakukan) terhadap mereka, sampai datang pertolongan Allah kepada mereka.' [Surat al-An'am: 34]. Semakin berat gangguan maka semakin dekat pertolongan Allah. Tidak berarti bahwa pertolongan Allah hanya dalam bentuk seseorang dibantu semasa hidupnya dan melihat bahwa dakwahnya telah menghasilkan hasil yang positif, melainkan pertolongan ini mungkin datang setelah kematiannya sehingga Allah menjadikan hati manusia menerima apa yang ia serukan, sehingga mereka mengikutinya dan berpegang teguh padanya. Ini juga dihitung sebagai pertolongan dan kemenangan dari Allah, meskipun itu terjadi setelah kematiannya. Maka pendakwah harus bersabar dalam dakwahnya dan terus menerus di atasnya; bersabar atas agama Allah, Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung, yang ia serukan; bersabar dalam menghadapi gangguan apa pun yang ia temui. Sesungguhnya para rasul menghadapi gangguan dan menyakitkan dari kata-kata dan perbuatan manusia. Allah berfirman: 'Demikianlah, tidak ada seorang rasulpun yang datang kepada orang-orang yang sebelum mereka melainkan mereka mengatakan: Ia adalah seorang tukang sihir atau seorang gila.' [Surat adz-Dzariyat: 52]. 'Dan seperti itulah, telah Kami adakan bagi tiap-tiap nabi, musuh dari orang-orang yang berdosa.' [Surat al-Furqan: 31]. Tetapi pendakwah harus menghadapi ini dengan kesabaran. Perhatikanlah firman Allah kepada Rasul-Nya: 'Sesungguhnya Kamilah yang menurunkan Al-Qur'an kepadamu (hai Muhammad) dengan berangsur-angsur.' [Surat al-Insan: 23]. Ini mengandung isyarat bahwa setiap orang yang berdiri dengan Al-Qur'an ini pasti akan menghadapi hal-hal yang mengharuskannya untuk sabar dan tabah. Juga perhatikanlah contoh Nabi ketika kaumnya memukul beliau dan menyebabkan darah mengalir darinya. Sementara beliau mengusap darah dari wajahnya beliau berkata: 'Ya Allah, ampunilah kaumku karena mereka tidak mengetahui.' [Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari no. 683]. Pendakwah harus bersabar dan tabah dengan mengharapkan pahala dari Allah.
content_en: And Allaah is free from all imperfections and partners, and I am free from those who associate anything in worship along with Him." [surah Yoosuf: 108] The certain knowledge (baseerah) is with regard to that which he calls to; and means that the caller has knowledge of the Sharee’ah rulings, and how the call is to be made, and knows about the condition of those to whom the call is made. The fields of da’wah are many: Calling to Allaah by giving speeches and lectures; calling to Allaah through writing articles; calling to Allaah by means of circles of knowledge; calling to Allaah buy writing books propagating the Deen; and calling to Allaah in gatherings established for particular purposes. If a person sits in a gathering where he has come due to an invitation to a meal, for example, then this is an opportunity to call to Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic. However it should be done in such a way as will not bore or irritate the people. This may be achieved by putting forward some matter of knowledge to those present so that a discussion may begin, and it is well known that discussions and questions and answers are great benefit in understanding what Allaah sent down to His Messenger and in causing others to understand it. It may be more effective than giving an uninterrupted speech or lecture. Calling to Allaah was the duty of the messengers, and was the way of those who followed them upon good. So when a person knows the one whom he has to worship, and knows his Prophet, and his Deen, and Allaah has favored him with that and guided him to it, then he should strive to save his brothers by calling them to Allaah and should be glad to hear of the good tidings which the Prophet gave to ‘Alee ibn Abee Taalib, radiyallaahu’anhu, on the day of Khaybar “Proceed without hurrying until you reach their territory. Then call them to Islaam, and inform them of the rights of Allaah, the Most High, binding upon them; for by Allaah, it is better for you that Allaah should guide a single man through you than red camels.” [sahih al Bukharee vol. 5, p. 368-369, #521]. He said in a narration reported by Muslim “Whoever calls to guidance then there is for him a reward like the rewards of those who follow him and that will reduce nothing from their rewards, and whoever calls to misguidance will have sin upon him like the sins of those who follow him, and that will reduce nothing from their sins.” [sahih Muslim vol. 3, p. 1050 #4665]. (iv) Patiently persevering and bearing any harm encountered upon that way. [14] ________________________________________________________ As Sabr (Patient perseverance) is to confine oneself to obedience to Allaah; and to withhold oneself from disobedience to Allaah, and from being angry with what Allaah has decreed. So one should always be eager and energetic in calling to Allaah’s Deen, even if faced with ill treatment. This is because causing harm to those who call to right is a trait found in mankind, except for those whom Allaah has guided. Allaah said to His Prophet “Indeed messengers before you, were denied and rejected but they patiently bore the denial, rejection and harm of their people until our aid came to them.” [surah al An’aam: 34] The more sever the harm becomes the nearer is Allaah’s aid. It is not the case that Allaah’s aid is only that a person is aided in his lifetime and sees that his da’wah has produced positive results, rather this aid may come after his death such that Allaah causes the peoples hearts to accept what he called to, so that they follow it and adhere to it. This is also counted as being help and a grant of victory from Allaah, even though it is after his death. So the caller should patiently persevere in his call and continue upon it; patiently persevering upon the Deen of Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, which he calls to; having patience in facing whatever harm he encounters. Indeed the messengers faced harm and hurt from the words and actions of the people. Allaah says “Likewise no messenger came to the previous people except that they said ‘He is a sorcerer, a madman.’” [surah adh Dhaariyaat: 52] “And for each of the prophets we have made an enemy from the idol worshippers.” [surah al Furqaan: 31] But the caller should face this with patient perseverance. See the saying of Allaah to His Messenger “Indeed it is We who have sent down this Qur’aan to you, gradually.” [surah al Insaan: 23] This contains an indication that everyone who stands with this Qur’aan must certainly face that which requires him to have patience and perseverance. Also consider the example of the Prophet when his people struck him and caused blood to flow from him. As he was wiping away the blood from his face he was saying “Oh Allaah, forgive my people for they do not know.” [Reported by al Bukharee #683]. The caller should have patience and persevere hoping for Allaah’s reward.
content_ar: And Allaah is free from all imperfections and partners, and I am free from those who associate anything in worship along with Him." [السورة Yoosuf: 108] The certain العلم (baseerah) is فيما يتعلق بـ that which he calls to; and means that the caller has العلم of the Sharee’ah rulings, and how the call is to be made, and knows about the condition of those to whom the call is made. The fields of da’wah are many: Calling to Allaah by giving speeches and lectures; calling to Allaah through writing articles; calling to Allaah by means of circles of العلم; calling to Allaah buy writing الكتب propagating the Deen; and calling to Allaah in gatherings established for particular purposes. إذا a person sits in a gathering where he has come بسبب an invitation to a meal, على سبيل المثال, then this is an opportunity to call to Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic. غير أن it should be done in such a الطريق as will not bore or irritate the people. This may be achieved by putting forward some matter of العلم to those present لذا that a discussion may begin, and it is well known that discussions and questions and answers are great benefit in الفهم what Allaah sent down to His Messenger and in causing others لفهم it. It may be more effective than giving an uninterrupted speech or lecture. Calling to Allaah was the الواجب of the الرسل, and was the الطريق of those who followed them upon الخير. لذا عندما a person knows the one whom he has للعبادة, and knows his النبي, and his Deen, and Allaah has favored him with that and guided him to it, then he should strive to save his brothers by calling them to Allaah and should be glad to hear of the الخير tidings which النبي ﷺ gave to ‘Alee ibn Abee Taalib, radiyallaahu’anhu, on the day of Khaybar “Proceed without hurrying حتى you reach their territory. Then call them to Islaam, and inform them of the الحقوق of Allaah, the Most High, binding upon them; for by Allaah, it is better for you that Allaah should guide a single man through you than red camels.” [صحيح al Bukharee vol. 5, p. 368-369, #521]. قال in a narration reported by مسلم “Whoever calls to الهداية then there is for him a الأجر like the rewards of those who follow him and that will reduce nothing from their rewards, and whoever calls to misguidance will have الذنب upon him like the الذنوب of those who follow him, and that will reduce nothing from their الذنوب.” [صحيح مسلم vol. 3, p. 1050 #4665]. (iv) Patiently persevering and bearing any harm encountered upon that الطريق. [14] ________________________________________________________ As الصبر (Patient perseverance) is to confine oneself to obedience to Allaah; and to withhold oneself from disobedience to Allaah, and from being angry with what Allaah has decreed. لذا ينبغي للمرء always be eager and energetic in calling to Allaah’s Deen, even إذا faced with ill treatment.
